sourceText: Late 19th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa Karne ya 19

sourceText: Nana Asma'u
translatedText: Nana Asma'u

sourceText: Witnessing the devastation of war during the Fulani Jihad and the widespread lack of education for rural women.
translatedText: Kushuhudia uharibifu wa vita wakati wa Jihad ya Fulani na ukosefu mkubwa wa elimu kwa wanawake wa vijijini.

sourceText: Using her position, scholarship, and poetry to create a sustainable educational system for women that spread knowledge and Islamic values.
translatedText: Kwa kutumia nafasi yake, usomi, na ushairi kuunda mfumo endelevu wa elimu kwa wanawake unaoeneza maarifa na maadili ya Kiislamu.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Facing immense resistance from the powerful priesthood and the populace for abandoning Egypt's traditional pantheon of gods in favor of the one god, the Aten.
translatedText: Inakabiliwa na upinzani mkubwa kutoka kwa ukuhani wenye nguvu na watu wengi kwa kuacha miungu ya kitamaduni ya Misri ili kupendelea mungu mmoja, Aten.

sourceText: Co-ruling with Akhenaten, we founded a new capital, Akhetaten (Amarna), and fostered a revolutionary new style of art and worship to solidify our vision for the Aten.
translatedText: Kwa kushirikiana na Akhenaten, tulianzisha mji mkuu mpya, Akhetaten (Amarna), na tukakuza mtindo mpya wa kimapinduzi wa sanaa na ibada ili kuimarisha maono yetu kwa Aten.

sourceText: New Kingdom of Egypt
translatedText: Ufalme Mpya wa Misri

sourceText: The murder of her husband by her own brother, Pygmalion, and the profound betrayal and abandonment by her lover, Aeneas.
translatedText: Mauaji ya mume wake na kaka yake mwenyewe, Pygmalion, na usaliti mkubwa na kuachwa na mpenzi wake, Aeneas.

sourceText: She channeled her grief into action, founding the great city of Carthage and becoming a capable leader. Faced with a second tragedy, she chose to preserve her honor through a defiant death.
translatedText: Alielekeza huzuni yake katika matendo, akaanzisha jiji kuu la Carthage na kuwa kiongozi mwenye uwezo. Alipokabiliwa na janga la pili, alichagua kuhifadhi heshima yake kupitia kifo cha ukaidi.

sourceText: Ancient Carthage (c. 8th Century BCE)
translatedText: Carthage ya Kale (karibu Karne ya 8 KK)

sourceText: Ranavalona I
translatedText: Ranavalona I

sourceText: Constant threats from European colonial powers and internal dissent
translatedText: Vitisho vya mara kwa mara kutoka kwa mamlaka ya kikoloni ya Ulaya na upinzani wa ndani

sourceText: Enforcing strict isolationism, persecuting Christians, and consolidating power through the tangena ordeal
translatedText: Kulazimisha utengano mkali, kuwatesa Wakristo, na kuunganisha nguvu kupitia jaribu la tangena

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Gebre Mesqel Lalibela
translatedText: Gebre Mesqel Bora Zaidi Kati Ya Gebre Mesqel

sourceText: Exiled due to a succession dispute and allegedly poisoned by his own half-sister, leading to a near-death experience.
translatedText: Alifukuzwa kwa sababu ya mzozo wa mrithi na kudaiwa kulishwa sumu na dada yake wa kambo, na kusababisha tukio la karibu kufa.

sourceText: His divine vision during his coma inspired him to return, claim the throne, and build the rock-hewn churches as an act of faith.
translatedText: Maono yake ya kimungu wakati wa kukosa fahamu yalimchochea kurudi, kudai kiti cha enzi, na kujenga makanisa yaliyochongwa kwenye miamba kama tendo la imani.

sourceText: 12th-13th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 12-13

sourceText: Leo Africanus
translatedText: Leo Africanus

sourceText: Exile from his homeland of Granada, capture by pirates, and a forced conversion to Christianity in a foreign land.
translatedText: Kuhamishwa kutoka nchi yake ya Granada, kutekwa na maharamia, na kulazimishwa kugeuzwa Ukristo katika nchi ya kigeni.

sourceText: He channeled his vast experiences and knowledge into his seminal work, 'Description of Africa', under the patronage of Pope Leo X, thus securing his legacy.
translatedText: Alielekeza tajriba na maarifa yake mengi katika kazi yake ya awali, 'Maelezo ya Afrika', chini ya uangalizi wa Papa Leo X, hivyo kupata urithi wake.

sourceText: 16th Century Renaissance
translatedText: Renaissance ya Karne ya 16

sourceText: Betrayed by deceptive treaties and overwhelmed by superior firepower, I witnessed the fall of my kingdom and the subjugation of my people.
translatedText: Nikiwa nimesalitiwa na mikataba ya udanganyifu na kuzidiwa nguvu na moto wa hali ya juu, nilishuhudia anguko la ufalme wangu na kutiishwa kwa watu wangu.

sourceText: Though my kingdom was lost, my story serves as a testament to the struggle for sovereignty and a warning against the insatiable greed of empire.
translatedText: Ingawa ufalme wangu ulipotea, hadithi yangu inatumika kama ushuhuda wa mapambano ya ukuu na onyo dhidi ya uroho usiotosheka wa himaya.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Imprisonment in his youth, internal power struggles, and the threat of European colonialism.
translatedText: Kifungo katika ujana wake, mapambano ya ndani ya madaraka, na tishio la ukoloni wa Ulaya.

sourceText: Consolidating power, unifying the nation, and achieving a decisive victory against a foreign invader.
translatedText: Kuimarisha mamlaka, kuunganisha taifa, na kupata ushindi wa uhakika dhidi ya mvamizi wa kigeni.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Faced constant warfare and external threats during the chaos of the 'Difaqane' (the Crushing) caused by the rise of the Zulu Empire.
translatedText: Ilikabiliwa na vita vya mara kwa mara na vitisho vya nje wakati wa machafuko ya 'Difaqane' (Kusagwa) iliyosababishwa na kuinuka kwa Dola ya Wazulu.

sourceText: Utilized strategic defense from his Thaba Bosiu mountain fortress, diplomatic negotiation, and ultimately secured his nation's survival by requesting British protection.
translatedText: Alitumia ulinzi wa kimkakati kutoka kwa ngome yake ya mlima wa Thaba Bosiu, mazungumzo ya kidiplomasia, na hatimaye kupata uhai wa taifa lake kwa kuomba ulinzi wa Uingereza.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Witnessing the corruption and oppressive taxes of the Turco-Egyptian rulers and the perceived decay of pure Islamic practice.
translatedText: Kushuhudia ufisadi na kodi dhalimu za watawala wa Turco-Misri na uozo unaoonekana kuwa wa utendaji safi wa Kiislamu.

sourceText: Embracing my divine calling as the Mahdi, unifying the tribes of Sudan under the banner of faith, and leading them to victory against foreign oppressors.
translatedText: Kukubali wito wangu wa kimungu kama Mahdi, kuyaunganisha makabila ya Sudan chini ya bendera ya imani, na kuwaongoza kwenye ushindi dhidi ya wadhalimu wa kigeni.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: The struggle against French colonialism, the fall of his kingdom, and his eventual exile and death far from his homeland.
translatedText: Mapambano dhidi ya ukoloni wa Ufaransa, kuanguka kwa ufalme wake, na hatimaye uhamisho wake na kifo mbali na nchi yake.

sourceText: Even in defeat and exile, he became an enduring symbol of African independence and anti-colonial resistance.
translatedText: Hata katika kushindwa na uhamishoni, akawa ishara ya kudumu ya uhuru wa Afrika na upinzani dhidi ya ukoloni.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Saad Zaghloul
translatedText: Saad Zaghloul

sourceText: Repeated exile and imprisonment by the British for his nationalist activities.
translatedText: Kuhamishwa mara kwa mara na kufungwa na Waingereza kwa shughuli zake za utaifa.

sourceText: Achieved Egypt's nominal independence through relentless negotiation and by galvanizing popular support.
translatedText: Ilifikia uhuru wa kawaida wa Misri kupitia mazungumzo yasiyokoma na kwa kuhimiza uungwaji mkono wa watu wengi.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwanzo wa Karne ya 20

sourceText: Samori Toure
translatedText: Samori Toure

sourceText: Fighting the relentless advance of French colonialism while managing internal rivalries and the immense suffering of my people during our long march.
translatedText: Kupambana na maendeleo yasiyokoma ya ukoloni wa Ufaransa huku nikisimamia mashindano ya ndani na mateso makubwa ya watu wangu wakati wa maandamano yetu marefu.

sourceText: Through faith in Allah, military innovation, strategic retreats, and the unwavering spirit of my people, I forged an empire and resisted a global power for nearly two decades.
translatedText: Kupitia imani kwa Mwenyezi Mungu, uvumbuzi wa kijeshi, mafungo ya kimkakati, na roho isiyoyumba ya watu wangu, nilitengeneza himaya na kupinga mamlaka ya kimataifa kwa karibu miongo miwili.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Samuel Ajayi Crowther
translatedText: Samuel Ajayi Crowther

sourceText: Enslavement and racial prejudice
translatedText: Utumwa na ubaguzi wa rangi

sourceText: Faith, education, and establishing identity through language
translatedText: Imani, elimu, na kuanzisha utambulisho kupitia lugha

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Sayyid Mohammed Abdullah Hassan
translatedText: Sayyid Mohammed Abdullah Hassan

sourceText: The division and oppression of his homeland by foreign colonial powers: the British, Italians, and Ethiopians.
translatedText: Mgawanyiko na ukandamizaji wa nchi yake na mamlaka ya kikoloni ya kigeni: Waingereza, Waitaliano, na Waethiopia.

sourceText: Unified Somali clans under the banner of Islam and nationalism, leading a 20-year armed resistance.
translatedText: Koo zilizoungana za Kisomali chini ya bendera ya Uislamu na utaifa, na kuongoza upinzani wa silaha wa miaka 20.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Constant conflict with and condemnation from the Islamic scholars of Timbuktu, who labeled me a tyrant.
translatedText: Migogoro ya mara kwa mara na kulaaniwa kutoka kwa wanazuoni wa Kiislamu wa Timbuktu, ambao walinitaja kuwa dhalimu.

sourceText: By building an unstoppable military force and balancing the needs of my rural, traditionalist base with the urban Muslim elite.
translatedText: Kwa kujenga jeshi lisilozuilika na kusawazisha mahitaji ya msingi wangu wa kijijini, wa kijadi na wasomi wa Kiislamu wa mijini.

sourceText: 15th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 15

sourceText: Sol Plaatje
translatedText: Picha ya Sol

sourceText: Witnessing the systematic dispossession and oppression of his people through discriminatory laws like the 1913 Natives' Land Act.
translatedText: Kushuhudia kunyang'anywa na kukandamizwa kwa watu wake kupitia sheria za kibaguzi kama vile Sheria ya Ardhi ya Wenyeji ya 1913.

sourceText: Using his skills as a writer, journalist, and diplomat to document injustices, raise international awareness, and organize political resistance.
translatedText: Akitumia ujuzi wake kama mwandishi, mwandishi wa habari, na mwanadiplomasia kuandika dhuluma, kuongeza ufahamu wa kimataifa, na kuandaa upinzani wa kisiasa.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Witnessing my country descend into civil war due to my religious policies, and the immense loss of life among my own people.
translatedText: Kushuhudia nchi yangu ikiingia kwenye vita vya wenyewe kwa wenyewe kwa sababu ya sera zangu za kidini, na upotezaji mkubwa wa maisha kati ya watu wangu mwenyewe.

sourceText: By revoking my decree and abdicating the throne in favor of my son, I chose the unity of my people over my personal convictions and power.
translatedText: Kwa kubatilisha amri yangu na kukataa kiti cha enzi kwa niaba ya mwanangu, nilichagua umoja wa watu wangu badala ya imani na uwezo wangu binafsi.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 17

sourceText: The encroachment of the Roman Empire and the personal injury I sustained in battle.
translatedText: Uvamizi wa Milki ya Roma na jeraha la kibinafsi nililopata katika vita.

sourceText: Leading my armies to a strategic stalemate and negotiating a peace treaty that respected our sovereignty.
translatedText: Kuongoza majeshi yangu kwenye mkwamo wa kimkakati na kujadili mkataba wa amani ambao uliheshimu uhuru wetu.

sourceText: 1st Century BCE
translatedText: Karne ya 1 KK

sourceText: Ahmadu Bamba
translatedText: Ahmadu Bamba

sourceText: Misunderstanding and persecution by French colonial authorities, leading to years of exile.
translatedText: Kutokuelewana na kuteswa na mamlaka ya kikoloni ya Ufaransa, na kusababisha miaka ya uhamishoni.

sourceText: Strength through faith, non-violent resistance, and building a community through education and labor.
translatedText: Nguvu kupitia imani, upinzani usio na vurugu, na kujenga jumuiya kupitia elimu na kazi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: The powerful resistance from the priesthood of Amun, the societal upheaval my reforms caused, and the eventual erasure of my legacy.
translatedText: Upinzani mkubwa kutoka kwa ukuhani wa Amun, msukosuko wa kijamii ambao mageuzi yangu yalisababisha, na hatimaye kufutwa kwa urithi wangu.

sourceText: My unwavering commitment to my new faith and the construction of a new capital, Akhetaten (Amarna), as the center of this new world order.
translatedText: Ahadi yangu isiyoyumba kwa imani yangu mpya na ujenzi wa mji mkuu mpya, Akhetaten (Amarna), kama kitovu cha utaratibu huu mpya wa ulimwengu.

sourceText: New Kingdom of Egypt, 18th Dynasty
translatedText: Ufalme Mpya wa Misri, Nasaba ya 18

sourceText: Ahmad Baba al-Massufi
translatedText: Ahmad Baba al-Massufi

sourceText: The destruction of Timbuktu and the Songhai Empire by the Moroccan invasion; his arrest, the confiscation of his library, and his long exile in Marrakesh.
translatedText: Kuharibiwa kwa Timbuktu na Dola ya Songhai na uvamizi wa Morocco; kukamatwa kwake, kunyang'anywa maktaba yake, na uhamisho wake wa muda mrefu huko Marrakesh.

sourceText: His continued scholarship even in exile, his eventual release, and his return to a devastated Timbuktu where he continued to teach until his death.
translatedText: Kuendelea kwake kimasomo hata uhamishoni, kuachiliwa kwake hatimaye, na kurudi kwake Timbuktu iliyoharibiwa ambako aliendelea kufundisha hadi kifo chake.

sourceText: 16th-17th Century Songhai Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Songhai ya Karne ya 16-17

sourceText: Ascending to the throne as a child, I ruled under the regency of my mother and faced the challenge of uniting a diverse kingdom while managing powerful external influences like the Roman Empire.
translatedText: Kupanda kiti cha enzi nikiwa mtoto, nilitawala chini ya utawala wa mama yangu na nikakabiliana na changamoto ya kuunganisha ufalme tofauti huku nikisimamia ushawishi wenye nguvu wa nje kama Milki ya Kirumi.

sourceText: Guided by my tutor, Frumentius, I embraced a new faith which unified my people. Through strategic military campaigns and by controlling vital trade routes, I solidified Aksum's power and legacy.
translatedText: Kwa kuongozwa na mwalimu wangu, Frumentius, nilikubali imani mpya iliyounganisha watu wangu. Kupitia kampeni za kimkakati za kijeshi na kwa kudhibiti njia muhimu za biashara, niliimarisha uwezo na urithi wa Aksum.

sourceText: 4th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 4

sourceText: Anton Wilhelm Amo
translatedText: Anton Wilhelm Amo

sourceText: Forcible displacement from his homeland, racial prejudice, and isolation after the death of his patron.
translatedText: Kuhamishwa kwa nguvu kutoka kwa nchi yake, ubaguzi wa rangi, na kutengwa baada ya kifo cha mlinzi wake.

sourceText: Achieving academic excellence through noble patronage, earning a doctorate in philosophy, and pioneering as a lecturer in European universities.
translatedText: Kufikia ubora wa kitaaluma kupitia ufadhili mzuri, kupata udaktari katika falsafa, na upainia kama mhadhiri katika vyuo vikuu vya Uropa.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 18

sourceText: Enduring five exiles and constant persecution from Roman emperors and Arian-sympathizing clergy for defending orthodox doctrine.
translatedText: Kuvumilia wahamishwa watano na mateso ya mara kwa mara kutoka kwa watawala wa Kirumi na makasisi wenye huruma ya Arian kwa kutetea fundisho la kiorthodox.

sourceText: Through unwavering conviction and prolific writing from exile, he rallied support for the Nicene cause, ultimately ensuring its triumph.
translatedText: Kupitia imani isiyoyumbayumba na maandishi mengi kutoka uhamishoni, alikusanya uungwaji mkono kwa sababu ya Nicene, hatimaye kuhakikisha ushindi wake.

sourceText: 4th Century Roman Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Kirumi ya Karne ya 4

sourceText: Intense theological battles, particularly with Nestorius, and navigating the political turmoil and violence of Alexandria.
translatedText: Vita vikali vya kitheolojia, haswa na Nestorius, na kuzunguka machafuko ya kisiasa na vurugu za Alexandria.

sourceText: Vindication at the Council of Ephesus, where my Christological views were upheld, solidifying orthodox doctrine for generations.
translatedText: Uthibitisho katika Baraza la Efeso, ambapo maoni yangu ya Kikristo yalizingatiwa, yakiimarisha fundisho la kiorthodox kwa vizazi.

sourceText: 5th Century Roman Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Kirumi ya Karne ya 5

sourceText: Amda Seyon I
translatedText: Amda Seyon I

sourceText: Constant threats from surrounding Muslim sultanates and internal rebellions from vassal lords.
translatedText: Vitisho vya mara kwa mara kutoka kwa masultani wa Kiislamu na uasi wa ndani kutoka kwa mabwana wa kibaraka.

sourceText: Through relentless military campaigns, strategic genius, and a policy of firm centralization, he crushed his enemies and expanded his empire.
translatedText: Kupitia kampeni za kijeshi zisizokoma, fikra za kimkakati, na sera ya kuweka serikali kuu imara, aliwakandamiza maadui zake na kupanua himaya yake.

sourceText: 14th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 14

sourceText: Abd al-Rahman al-Jabarti
translatedText: Abd al-Rahman al-Jabarti

sourceText: Witnessing the violent disruption of his society by foreign invasion and the subsequent brutal power struggles.
translatedText: Kushuhudia kuvurugwa kwa jeuri kwa jamii yake kwa uvamizi wa kigeni na mapambano ya kikatili ya madaraka yaliyofuata.

sourceText: By meticulously documenting events, he preserved the memory of his time and provided a powerful Egyptian perspective on a pivotal historical moment.
translatedText: Kwa kuandika matukio kwa uangalifu, alihifadhi kumbukumbu ya wakati wake na kutoa mtazamo wenye nguvu wa Misri juu ya wakati muhimu wa kihistoria.

sourceText: Late 18th - Early 19th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 18 - Mapema karne ya 19

sourceText: Abd al-Mu'min
translatedText: Abd al-Mu'min

sourceText: The immense burden of succeeding my master, Ibn Tumart, and the decades-long struggle to unite warring tribes and overthrow the decadent Almoravids.
translatedText: Mzigo mkubwa wa kumrithi bwana wangu, Ibn Tumart, na mapambano ya miongo kadhaa ya kuunganisha makabila yanayopigana na kuwapindua Almoravids waliokufa.

sourceText: Through unwavering faith in the Almohad doctrine, strategic patience, and relentless military campaigns, I forged unity from chaos and built a righteous empire.
translatedText: Kupitia imani isiyoyumba katika fundisho la Almohad, subira ya kimkakati, na kampeni za kijeshi zisizokoma, nilitengeneza umoja kutoka kwa machafuko na kujenga himaya ya haki.

sourceText: 12th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 12

sourceText: The loss of my country's independence and the long years of imprisonment and exile.
translatedText: Kupoteza uhuru wa nchi yangu na miaka mingi ya kifungo na uhamishoni.

sourceText: Through unwavering faith, devotion to scholarship, and embracing a universal humanism that transcended conflict.
translatedText: Kupitia imani isiyoyumba, kujitolea kwa usomi, na kukumbatia ubinadamu wa ulimwengu wote uliovuka migogoro.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Facing the brutality of the apartheid system, including being deposed as a chief, repeated arrests, and severe restrictions on my movement.
translatedText: Kukabiliwa na ukatili wa mfumo wa ubaguzi wa rangi, ikiwa ni pamoja na kuondolewa kama chifu, kukamatwa mara kwa mara, na vikwazo vikali kwa harakati zangu.

sourceText: Drawing strength from my Christian faith and the unwavering spirit of my people, I led the ANC with a steadfast commitment to non-violent resistance.
translatedText: Nikipata nguvu kutoka kwa imani yangu ya Kikristo na roho isiyoyumba ya watu wangu, niliongoza ANC kwa kujitolea thabiti kwa upinzani usio na vurugu.

sourceText: 20th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 20

sourceText: Yaa Asantewaa
translatedText: Ewe Asantewaa

sourceText: The arrogance of the British governor, the demand for our sacred Golden Stool, and the initial fear of our chiefs.
translatedText: Kiburi cha gavana wa Uingereza, hitaji la Kinyesi chetu kitakatifu cha Dhahabu, na woga wa awali wa wakuu wetu.

sourceText: By rallying the chiefs and the people, I took command of our army and led the fight to defend our sovereignty and culture.
translatedText: Kwa kuwakusanya machifu na wananchi, nilichukua uongozi wa jeshi letu na kuongoza mapambano ya kutetea enzi kuu na utamaduni wetu.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 19 - Mapema karne ya 20

sourceText: Suffering persecution and torture for his faith, and the posthumous condemnation of his teachings as heresy.
translatedText: Kuteswa na kuteswa kwa ajili ya imani yake, na kulaaniwa baada ya kifo cha mafundisho yake kama uzushi.

sourceText: Through immense scholarly dedication, he produced foundational works of theology and biblical criticism, leaving a profound intellectual and spiritual legacy.
translatedText: Kupitia kujitolea sana kwa kitaaluma, alitayarisha kazi za msingi za theolojia na ukosoaji wa Biblia, akiacha urithi mkubwa wa kiakili na kiroho.

sourceText: 3rd Century Roman Empire
translatedText: Milki ya Kirumi ya Karne ya 3

sourceText: Osei Tutu I
translatedText: Osei Tutu I

sourceText: The disunity and subjugation of the Akan peoples under the dominant Denkyira kingdom.
translatedText: Mfarakano na kutiishwa kwa watu wa Akan chini ya ufalme mkuu wa Denkyira.

sourceText: Forging military alliances, creating a constitution, and establishing the Golden Stool as a unifying symbol of divine authority.
translatedText: Kuanzisha ushirikiano wa kijeshi, kuunda katiba, na kuanzisha Kinyesi cha Dhahabu kama ishara ya kuunganisha ya mamlaka ya kimungu.

sourceText: Late 17th - Early 18th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 17 - Mapema karne ya 18

sourceText: Olaudah Equiano
translatedText: Olaudah Equiano

sourceText: Kidnapped from my home in Africa, I endured the unimaginable brutality of the Middle Passage and the dehumanization of slavery.
translatedText: Nikiwa nimetekwa nyara kutoka nyumbani kwangu barani Afrika, nilivumilia ukatili usiowazika wa Njia ya Kati na kudhoofisha utu wa utumwa.

sourceText: Through diligence, trade, and a profound faith, I purchased my freedom and used the power of my own story to fight for the abolition of the slave trade.
translatedText: Kupitia bidii, biashara, na imani kubwa, nilinunua uhuru wangu na kutumia uwezo wa hadithi yangu mwenyewe kupigania kukomesha biashara ya watumwa.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 18

sourceText: Constant foreign invasions and internal rivalries. My reign was a perpetual state of war against Egyptians, Italians, and the Mahdists of Sudan.
translatedText: Uvamizi wa mara kwa mara wa kigeni na mashindano ya ndani. Utawala wangu ulikuwa ni hali ya kudumu ya vita dhidi ya Wamisri, Waitalia, na Wamahd wa Sudan.

sourceText: Through military prowess and shrewd diplomacy, I preserved Ethiopia's sovereignty and forged a stronger national identity rooted in our shared Orthodox faith.
translatedText: Kupitia uwezo wa kijeshi na diplomasia ya busara, nilihifadhi uhuru wa Ethiopia na kutengeneza utambulisho wenye nguvu zaidi wa kitaifa unaotokana na imani yetu ya pamoja ya Othodoksi.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 19

sourceText: Usman dan Fodio
translatedText: Usman na Fodio

sourceText: Witnessing the corruption, syncretic religious practices, and unjust rule of the Hausa city-states, which led to persecution and exile for my community.
translatedText: Kushuhudia ufisadi, mazoea ya kidini, na utawala usio wa haki wa majimbo ya jiji la Hausa, ambayo ilisababisha mnyanyaso na uhamisho kwa jamii yangu.

sourceText: Leading a successful jihad (the Fulani War) against the Hausa rulers and establishing the Sokoto Caliphate, a new state founded on Islamic law and principles of social justice.
translatedText: Kuongoza jihad yenye mafanikio (Vita vya Fulani) dhidi ya watawala wa Kihausa na kuanzisha Ukhalifa wa Sokoto, dola mpya iliyosimikwa kwa sheria za Kiislamu na kanuni za uadilifu wa kijamii.

sourceText: Late 18th - Early 19th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 18 - Mapema karne ya 19

sourceText: The relentless encroachment of Portuguese slavers, internal betrayals, and the personal loss of her family to the conflict.
translatedText: Uvamizi usiokoma wa watumwa wa Ureno, usaliti wa ndani, na upotezaji wa kibinafsi wa familia yake kwenye mzozo.

sourceText: Through brilliant military alliances, masterful diplomacy, and an unwavering resolve to protect her people's sovereignty.
translatedText: Kupitia ushirikiano mzuri wa kijeshi, diplomasia ya ustadi, na azimio lisiloyumbayumba la kulinda enzi kuu ya watu wake.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 17

sourceText: Idris Alooma
translatedText: Idris Alooma

sourceText: Inheriting a fractured empire, facing threats from neighboring groups, and commanding an outdated army.
translatedText: Kurithi ufalme uliovunjika, kukabili vitisho kutoka kwa vikundi vya jirani, na kuamuru jeshi lililopitwa na wakati.

sourceText: By modernizing the military with Ottoman firearms, centralizing administration under Islamic law, and conducting strategic campaigns to unify and expand the empire.
translatedText: Kwa kufanya jeshi kuwa la kisasa kwa kutumia silaha za moto za Ottoman, kuweka utawala kati chini ya sheria ya Kiislamu, na kufanya kampeni za kimkakati za kuunganisha na kupanua himaya.

sourceText: Late 16th - Early 17th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa 16 - Mapema karne ya 17

sourceText: As a commoner by birth, I had to navigate the rigid hierarchy of the royal court and prove my worth through sheer intellect and skill.
translatedText: Nikiwa mtu wa kawaida kwa kuzaliwa, ilinibidi niende kwenye uongozi mgumu wa mahakama ya kifalme na kuthibitisha thamani yangu kupitia akili na ustadi mtupu.

sourceText: My service to the Pharaoh was so valued that I was granted honors typically reserved for royalty, eventually being deified as a god of medicine and wisdom.
translatedText: Utumishi wangu kwa Farao ulithaminiwa sana hivi kwamba nilipewa heshima ambazo kwa kawaida zilitengwa kwa ajili ya wafalme, na hatimaye nikafanywa kuwa mungu wa dawa na hekima.

sourceText: Old Kingdom of Egypt (c. 27th Century BCE)
translatedText: Ufalme wa Kale wa Misri (C. Karne ya 27 KK)

sourceText: Witnessing the religious laxity and anthropomorphism prevalent under the Almoravid dynasty.
translatedText: Kushuhudia ulegevu wa kidini na anthropomorphism iliyoenea chini ya nasaba ya Almoravid.

sourceText: By formulating a strict doctrine of divine unity (Tawhid) and uniting Berber tribes into a powerful religio-political force.
translatedText: Kwa kuunda fundisho kali la umoja wa kimungu (Tawhid) na kuunganisha makabila ya Waberber kuwa nguvu yenye nguvu ya kidini-kisiasa.

sourceText: 12th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 12

sourceText: Facing the constant threat of war, the challenge of being a female ruler in a patriarchal era, and the burden of defending and expanding her kingdom.
translatedText: Kukabiliana na tishio la mara kwa mara la vita, changamoto ya kuwa mtawala mwanamke katika enzi ya mfumo dume, na mzigo wa kutetea na kupanua ufalme wake.

sourceText: Through unmatched military skill, strategic genius, and inspiring leadership, she not only defended her kingdom but significantly expanded its influence and wealth.
translatedText: Kupitia ustadi wa kijeshi usio na kifani, fikra za kimkakati, na uongozi wa kutia moyo, hakutetea tu ufalme wake bali alipanua kwa kiasi kikubwa ushawishi na utajiri wake.

sourceText: Late 16th Century
translatedText: Mwisho wa karne ya 16

sourceText: Forced into solitude, hiding in a cave for two years to escape religious persecution.
translatedText: Kulazimishwa kuwa peke yake, kujificha katika pango kwa miaka miwili ili kuepuka mateso ya kidini.

sourceText: Used his isolation to develop a philosophy of reason, concluding all humans are equal and recording his thoughts in the 'Hatata'.
translatedText: Alitumia kutengwa kwake kukuza falsafa ya akili, akihitimisha kuwa wanadamu wote ni sawa na kurekodi mawazo yake katika 'Hatata'.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karne ya 17
